page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC Genoa cores running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU resources are allocated on a shared basis. The hardware configuration is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled as a 480 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks reveal a significant divergence between single-threaded and multi-threaded capabilities. The single-core CPU performance under stress-ng is highly competitive, ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show strong results, placing in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core CPU performance drops to average levels, and the overall PassMark CPU score is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also weak, though large block read and write operations maintain average throughput.

The shared CPU allocation introduces notable performance tradeoffs for concurrent or high-throughput workloads. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all perform poorly, with web serving falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarks. Large language model inference is also limited, with a medium 7B model achieving only 4.99 tokens per second. Consequently, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy but compute-light applications, single-threaded data processing, and staging environments, rather than high-traffic web applications or intensive production databases.
description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 shared vCPUs on AMD EPYC Genoa physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM and 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU capability, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside strong single-threaded gzip and bzip3 performance. However, the shared CPU allocation introduces performance tradeoffs, as evidenced by weak database operations, weak Redis SET throughput, and poor static web serving performance in the bottom 10%. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-demanding, non-parallelized workloads. It is best suited for single-threaded data processing, memory-heavy testing, and lightweight background utilities rather than high-traffic web hosting or intensive database applications.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC Genoa instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 480 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance but weak database and web serving throughput.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 480 GB NVMe storage, optimized for memory-heavy, single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A shared AMD Genoa instance pairing strong single-core processing with high memory capacity and local NVMe storage for non-parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB system memory and 480 GB local NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong single-threaded compression performance
Weak database operations, Redis throughput, and static web serving
Suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded tasks and development environments
